title: Identity Spoofing
text: Identity Spoofing Identity spoofing, or IP address spoofing, occurs when an attacker determines the IP address of a network, computer, or network component, and uses it to gain access or submit data under that identity, without being authorized to do so. Defenses To prevent this type of attack, take precautions at the network level: Ensure there are proper access control lists (ACLs) that implement ingress filtering. Implement authentication and encryption. Related Links Authentication for Web Applications Authentication is intended to ensure that users (or computers) accessing the system or application are who they purport to be. Authentication systems range in strength from simple user and password validation to transaction signing. Data Leaks Data leaks can result from incorrectly specified cookies, unverified client input, and verbose error messages. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
